title: Onagra mollissima
intro: Onagra mollissima, also known as evening primrose, is a perennial herbaceous plant that belongs to the evening primrose family (Onagraceae). The plant is valued for its edible seeds, which contain a high amount of gamma-linolenic acid (GLA), a polyunsaturated omega-6 fatty acid with anti-inflammatory and moisturizing properties, providing relief from conditions such as eczema, arthritis, and menopausal symptoms. The bioactive constituents of Onagra mollissima include GLA, as well as other antioxidants and phytosterols, which contribute to its potential health benefits. Evening primrose oil, extracted from the seeds, is commonly used in herbal preparations, such as capsules, softgels, and topical creams, which are often used to support skin health and alleviate symptoms of various inflammatory conditions.
taxonomy: Onagra mollissima is a species of flowering plant in the evening primrose family, Oenotheraceae. It is native to western North America, and its classification is as follows: the genus Onagra is typically classified within the tribe Onagreae, which is part of the subfamily Onagroideae. The family Oenotheraceae is a member of the order Myrtales. Onagra mollissima is further classified as a member of the class Magnoliopsida and the kingdom Plantae. This classification is based on the plant's morphological and anatomical characteristics.
